meta.description: Sheikha Cloud Sovereignty Framework — the binding legal and technical standard for all member states

principles.title: Data Residency
principles.rule: Data of citizens and government entities of a member state MUST be stored and processed within approved regions for that state. No data shall egress to non-approved jurisdictions without explicit consent and audit trail.
principles.enforcement: OPA policy + Kubernetes admission webhook + network egress rules

principles.title: Encryption Sovereignty
principles.rule: Each member state holds its own HSM-backed root encryption key (FIPS 140-3 Level 4). No other party — including Sheikha Consortium — can decrypt state-specific data without the state's key custodian.
principles.enforcement: HashiCorp Vault Enterprise + HSM integration per region

principles.title: Zero Trust Access
principles.rule: All access to cloud resources requires continuous verification of identity, device posture, and network context. No implicit trust — every request is authenticated and authorized.
principles.enforcement: Keycloak OIDC + OPA Gatekeeper + Istio mTLS

principles.title: Shariah Compliance
principles.rule: All commercial and financial operations on the platform must pass Shariah compliance checks. Prohibited activities (riba, gharar, maysir) are blocked at the API gateway level.
principles.enforcement: Sheikha Halal Engine + AI compliance checks

principles.title: Audit Immutability
principles.rule: All administrative actions, data access events, and financial transactions are recorded in an immutable blockchain audit log accessible to authorized state auditors.

principles.title: Open Governance
principles.rule: Platform policies and technical standards are decided by the Sheikha Cloud Council (one vote per member state). No single entity may unilaterally alter core platform behaviour.
principles.enforcement: Smart contract voting on Hyperledger Fabric

dataResidencyMatrix.description: Maps country codes to their approved processing regions

hsmConfig.keyEscrow: NONE — each state is sole custodian
